heading: Fees paid by residents
label: 414 Fees paid by residents
notes: Amendments2021—Subsec. (c)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “The fee shall be fixed as a percentage of the monthly income and monthly payments (including Federal payments) received by a resident. The percentage shall be the same for each facility of the Retirement Home. The Secretary of Defense may make any adjustment in a percentage that the Secretary determines appropriate.”Pub. L. 116–283, § 1412(b)(1)Subsec. (c)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 116–283, § 1412(b)(2)2011—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Administrator” for “Director”.Pub. L. 112–81, § 564(b)(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Administrators” for “Directors”.Pub. L. 112–81, § 564(b)(2)Subsec. (c)(3). , struck out at end “The first increase in a limitation on maximum monthly amount shall take effect on .”Pub. L. 112–81, § 565(a)January 1, 2003Subsec. (d). , struck out subsec. (d) which related to transitional fee structures.Pub. L. 112–81, § 565(b)2001— reenacted section catchline without change and amended text generally, substituting present provisions for provisions relating to collection of monthly fees in subsec. (a), deposit of fees in subsec. (b), fixing fees in subsec. (c), and application of fees in subsec. (d).Pub. L. 107–1071994—Subsec. (c)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “The fee shall be fixed as a percentage of Federal payments made to a resident, including monthly retired or retainer pay, monthly civil service annuity, monthly compensation or pension paid to the resident by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, and Social Security payments. Residents who do not receive such Federal payments shall be required to pay a monthly fee that is equivalent to the average monthly fee paid by residents who receive Federal payments, subject to such adjustments in the fee as the Retirement Home Board may make. The percentage shall be the same for each establishment of the Retirement Home.”Pub. L. 103–337, § 371(b)(1)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d) and struck out former subsec. (d) which specified fees to be paid by residents of the Naval Home and residents of the United States Soldiers’ and Airmen’s Home who became residents of the Retirement Home on the effective date specified in .Pub. L. 103–337, § 371(b)(2)section 1541(a) of Pub. L. 101–510Subsec. (e). , struck out subsec. (e) which read as follows: “A person who becomes a resident of the Retirement Home after the effective date specified in section 1541(a) shall be required to pay a monthly fee that is equal to 25 percent of Federal payments made to the resident, subject to such adjustments in the fee as may be made under subsection (c) of this section.”Pub. L. 103–337, § 371(b)(2)(A)

Effective Date of 1994 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title III, § 371(d)(2)108 Stat. 2735Oct. 5, 1994“The amendments made by subsection (b) [amending this section] shall take effect on .”October 1, 1997
Effective DateSection effective one year after , see , formerly set out as a note under .Nov. 5, 1990section 1541(a) of Pub. L. 101–510section 401 of this title

Relief for Residents of the Armed Forces Retirement Home Impacted by Increase in Fees, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title XIV, § 1415132 Stat. 2092Aug. 13, 2018“(a).—Prohibition on Removal for Inability To Pay Fee IncreaseA resident of the Armed Forces Retirement Home as of , may not be removed or released from the Retirement Home after that date based solely upon the inability of the resident to pay the amount of any increase in fees applicable to residents of the Retirement Home that takes effect on .September 30, 2018October 1, 2018“(b).—Other ReliefThe Chief Operating Officer of the Armed Forces Retirement Home shall take all actions practicable to accommodate residents of the Retirement Home who are impacted by the fee structure applicable to residents of the Retirement Home that takes effect on , including through hardship relief, additional deductions from gross income, and other appropriate actions.”October 1, 2018

Limitation on Applicability of Fee Increase for Residents of the Armed Forces Retirement Home, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title XIV, § 1416132 Stat. 2092Aug. 13, 2018“(a).—In GeneralIn the case of an individual who was a resident of the Armed Forces Retirement Home as of , the increase in fees for residents of the Home scheduled to take effect on , shall occur on an incremental basis over the three-year period beginning on , such that the total fee for such individual as a resident of the Home as of the end of such period covers the cost of care of such individual as a resident of the Home.April 9, 2018October 1, 2018October 1, 2018“(b).—Notice and Wait on Implementation of Future IncreasesAny increase in the fees for residents of the Home that is scheduled to take effect after , may not take effect until 90 days after the date on which a report on the increase is submitted to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives.”October 1, 2018

children.heading: Monthly fees
children.label: a Monthly fees
children.text: The Administrator of each facility of the Retirement Home shall collect a monthly fee from each resident of that facility.

children.heading: Deposit of fees
children.label: b Deposit of fees
children.text: The Administrators shall deposit fees collected under subsection (a) in the Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund.

The Chief Operating Officer, with the approval of the Secretary of Defense, shall from time to time prescribe the fees required by subsection (a). Changes to such fees shall be based on the financial needs of the Retirement Home and the ability of the residents to pay. A change of a fee may not take effect until 120 days after the Secretary of Defense transmits a notification of the change to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: The fee shall be fixed as a percentage of the monthly income and monthly payments (including Federal payments) received by a resident. The percentage shall be the same for each facility of the Retirement Home. The Secretary of Defense may make any adjustment in a percentage that the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: B
children.text: The calculation of monthly income and monthly payments under subparagraph (A) for a resident eligible under shall not be less than the retirement pay for equivalent active duty service as determined by the Chief Operating Officer, except as the Chief Operating Officer may provide because of the compelling personal circumstances of such resident.section 412(a)(5) of this title

The fee shall be subject to a limitation on maximum monthly amount. The amount of the limitation shall be increased, effective on January 1 of each year, by the percentage of the increase in retired pay and retainer pay that takes effect on the preceding December 1 under subsection (b) of without regard to paragraph (3) of such subsection.section 1401a of title 10

The Administrator of each facility of the Retirement Home may collect a fee upon admission from a resident accepted under equal to the deductions then in effect under for each year of service computed under chapter 1223 of title 10 and shall deposit such fee in the Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund.section 412(a)(5) of this titlesection 1007(i)(1) of title 37
